title: Emergency fund
description: Practice building an emergency fund from living expenses.

assessment_items.question_content:
An emergency fund helps you keep your home and pay essentials like rent or mortgage, car payments, and utilities during emergencies.

**How much should you aim for in your emergency fund savings?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: at least three months of essential expenses

assessment_items.question_content:
An emergency fund helps you keep your home and pay essentials like rent or mortgage, car payments, and utilities during emergencies.

**To start building an emergency fund,  which type of expenses should you start adding up first?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The emergency fund's purpose is to help offset unplanned expenses. 

**Which of these is an example of an unplanned expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

When saving money, it is important to pay attention to both planned, and unplanned expenses.

**Which of these is a planned expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are building your emergency fund and are writing down your monthly expenses:

Expense | Amount 
- | -: 
Rent | $\$1{,}050$
Utilities | $\$208$ 
Car payment | $\$453$ 
Insurance | $\$85$
Groceries | $\$600$
Phone & internet | $\$104$
**TOTAL** | $\$2{,}500$

Your goal is to have six months of expenses in your emergency fund. 

**How much money should you have in your emergency fund to meet your goal?** 

$\$$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We can multiply the amount of total expenses by the number of months.
You should have $\$15{,}000$ in your emergency fund to meet your goal.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sofia is working on her emergency fund. She is writing down all her monthly living expenses and adding them together.

Expense | Amount 
- | -: 
Rent | $\$1{,}200$
Utilities | $\$380$ 
Car payment | $\$525$ 
Insurance | $\$140$
Groceries | $\$820$
Dining out | $\$135$
**TOTAL** | $\$3{,}200$

However, Sofia has made a mistake and listed an expense that should not be listed.  

**Which expense should not be considered when making an emergency fund?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Ricky is working on his emergency fund. He is writing down all his monthly living expenses and is totaling them up. 

Expense | Amount 
- | -:

Utilities | $\$220$ 
Car payment | $\$525$ 
Entertainment | $\$140$
Groceries | $\$820$

However, Ricky has made a mistake and listed an expense that should not be listed.  

**Which expense should not be considered when making an emergency fund?**

**Which expense should not be considered when making an emergency fund?**

Sara is working on her emergency fund. She is writing down all her monthly living expenses and is totaling them up. 

Expense | Amount 
- | -:

Utilities | $\$480$ 
Car payment | $\$525$ 
Hair salon | $\$40$
Insurance | $\$820$
Groceries | $\$135$
**TOTAL** | $\$3{,}000$

However, Sara has made a mistake and listed an expense that should not be listed.  

**Which expense should not be considered when making an emergency fund?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You're working on building your emergency fund. Your total living expenses are $\$2{,}200$ per month. You're hoping to save enough for six months of expenses, and you're currently saving $\$1{,}100$ each month.   

**How long will it take you to fill your emergency fund if you continue saving at the same rate?**
  
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints: To find how long it will take you to fill your emergency fund, we should find out how much money you want in your emergency fund first.

You have $\$2{,}200$ a month in living expenses and want to have $6$ months worth of expenses saved.

$\$2{,}200 \times 6= \$13{,}200$

You want to save $\$13{,}200$ in your emergency fund.

You plan to save $\$1{,}100$ a month, every month. So, we can divide your emergency fund goal by the monthly savings amount.

$\$13{,}200\div\$1{,}100 = 12\text{ months}$
It will take you $12$ months to fill your emergency fund.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are working on building your emergency fund. Your total living expenses are $\$1{,}950$ per month and you are hoping to save enough for six months' worth of expenses. You are currently saving $\$900$ each month.  
 
**How long will it take you to fully fund your emergency fund?**
  
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints: To find how long it will take you to fill your emergency fund, we should find out how much money you want in your emergency fund first.

You have $\$1{,}950$ a month in living expenses and want to have $6$ months worth of expenses saved.

$\$1{,}950 \times 6= \$11{,}700$

You want to save $\$11{,}700$ in your emergency fund.

You plan to save $\$900$ a month, every month. So, we can divide your emergency fund goal by the monthly savings amount.

$\$11{,}700\div \$900 = 13\text{ months}$
It will take you $13$ months to fill your emergency fund.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are working on building your emergency fund. Your total living expenses are $\$1{,}800$ per month and you're hoping to save enough for three months' worth of expenses. You're currently saving $\$600$ each month.
   
**How long will it take you to fully fund your emergency fund?**
  
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints: To find how long it will take you to fill your emergency fund, we should find out how much money you want in your emergency fund first.

You have $\$1{,}800$ a month in living expenses and want to have $3$ months worth of expenses saved.

$\$1{,}800 \times 3= \$5{,}400$

You want to save $\$5{,}400$ in your emergency fund.

You plan to save $\$600$ a month, every month. So, we can divide your emergency fund goal by the monthly savings amount.

$\$5{,}400\div \$600 = 9\text{ months}$
It will take you $9$ months to fill your emergency fund.

assessment_items.question_content:
You're working on building your emergency fund. Your total living expenses are $\$3{,}900$ per month and you're hoping to save enough for three months' worth of expenses. You are currently saving $\$900$ each month. 
  
**How long will it take you to fully fund your emergency fund?**
  
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints: To find how long it will take you to fill your emergency fund, we should find out how much money you want in your emergency fund first.

You have $\$3{,}900$ a month in living expenses and want to have $3$ months worth of expenses saved.

$\$3{,}900 \times 3= \$11{,}700$

You want to save $\$11{,}700$ in your emergency fund.

You plan to save $\$900$ a month, every month. So, we can divide your emergency fund goal by the monthly savings amount.

$\$11{,}700\div \$900 = 13\text{ months}$
It will take you $13$ months to fill your emergency fund.
